topic: Someone discovers their friend has been lying about their job
raw_output:
[story 1]

My palms were slick against the worn wooden table as I stared at the documents spread before me. Liam was telling me, with that familiar, breezy confidence, that he was a senior architect at a prestigious downtown firm. But these pay stubs, the official-looking letterhead, they didn't match the glossy brochures he’d shown me. A tiny smudge on the corner of the contract, a date that was clearly too recent for the timeline he’d described. A slow, thrilling warmth began to spread through my chest, a rapid thrumming that made my breath catch in my throat. I leaned closer, my eyes darting between the paper and Liam’s suddenly very pale face. "Wait," I managed, my voice coming out higher than intended. "Where exactly is this office located?"

[story 2]

The air in the coffee shop suddenly felt too thin. Sarah kept tapping her pen against her notebook, a frantic rhythm that mirrored the quickening pulse in her neck. She’d been so assured, so animated, describing the thrilling world of corporate mergers and high-rise construction. Now, the details felt brittle, like thin ice about to crack. I noticed the way her gaze flickered away whenever I asked about specific project names. A sharp intake of breath escaped her lips. "I just... I need to confirm something," she murmured, her voice tight, avoiding eye contact. The sheer tension radiating off her was palpable, and watching her try to smooth over the discrepancy only made my own internal energy surge, a bright, undeniable lift in my spirit.

[story 3]

I found the discrepancy tucked away in an email chain—a subtle mismatch in the jargon used. My fingers flew across the keyboard, the keys clicking with an almost manic energy. Every line of text confirmed my suspicion, building a cascade of undeniable proof. A wide grin stretched across my face, a genuine, unrestrained expression. I looked up at Mark, who was leaning back in his chair, trying to appear nonchalant. "You're kidding me," I said, the sound vibrating with sheer delight. "You're not actually working at Sterling & Finch? You’re doing something completely different." The way his jaw tightened, the sudden flush creeping up his neck, told me everything I needed to know.
